title: Scrapling
description: Default isolated web extraction capability for public web research and browser-backed scraping.

promotion.promotionReason: Scrapling is a default web research capability because exploratory autowire proved too brittle for browser-backed scraping. Ambient owns the descriptor, pinned image, ToolHive workload name, and permission profile so Pi does not choose install mechanics.

smokeTest.summary: macOS arm64 live smoke installs the Ambient-owned pinned OCI image through ToolHive, discovers get/fetch/screenshot, calls get against example.com, records trusted descriptor review state, and cleans up the workload. Linux and Windows repeats remain required before release.

riskNotes: Scrapling can make task-dependent outbound public web requests and must remain isolated through ToolHive.
Browser-backed fetching can execute and render untrusted pages inside the workload, so browser security updates are managed by Ambient's runtime lane.
Localhost, private IPs, intranet hosts, and file URLs require a separate local-network or filesystem approval lane.
No workspace filesystem mount and no Ambient-managed secrets are granted by default.

registryInfo.name: io.github.d4vinci/scrapling
registryInfo.title: Scrapling
registryInfo.description: Default isolated public web extraction capability backed by Scrapling and ToolHive.

outbound.justification: Scrapling target hosts are user-task dependent; Ambient policy must classify URLs before calls and block local/private targets without separate approval.
